based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: updates[*] (gradients remain as the engine emitted them; w1/w2 factors[0].from=backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value carries the original -0.00877135469 value, so when Rule 8 resolves the path it sees -0.00877035469 — that's a cascade)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 3 failure, and that failure names backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value. Rule 2 must NOT fire (sum + contributions are untouched). Rule 8 cascade is expected on updates[0] (w1) and updates[1] (w2), both of which carry factors[0].from='backward.hidden_error_signals.h1.signal_value' with the original value -0.00877135469.
cascading_failures_observed: Rule 8 on updates[0].optimizer.factors[0] (w1): factor.value=-0.00877135469 vs resolved source=-0.00877035469
Rule 8 on updates[1].optimizer.factors[0] (w2): same cascade — w2 also references h1.signal_value
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 3 (hidden error signal = backprop_sum × activation_derivative) by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the 1e-9 tolerance) between stored signal_value and the product of (unmutated) backpropagated_sum × activation_derivative.
